content: When diluted $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ is added to aqueous solution of potassium chromate, yellow colour solution turns to orange colour. This signifies that

options.content: monocentric complex is converted into dicentric complex.
oxygen gel removed from chromate ions.

explanation:
On acidification of potassium chromate solution, yellow colour changes to orange due to reformation of dichromate. This suggests that monocentric complex, i.e., potassium monochromate is converted into dicentric complex, i.e. potassium dichromate. The complete reaction is as follows.

$\underset{\begin{array}{l}\text { Potassium } \\ \text { chromate }\end{array}}{2 \mathrm{~K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \longrightarrow \underset{\begin{array}{c}\text { Potassium } \\ \text { dichromate }\end{array}}{\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7}+\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

content: In the reaction, $$\mathrm{H}_2+\mathrm{I}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}$$. In a 4L flask, 0.8 mole of each $$\mathrm{H}_2$$ and $$\mathrm{I}_2$$ are taken. All equilibrium, 1 mole of $$\mathrm{HI}$$ is formed. What will be the value of equilibrium constant $$K_C$$ ?
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} K_C & =\frac{[\mathrm{HI}]^2}{\left[\mathrm{H}_2\right]\left[\mathrm{I}_2\right]}=\frac{(1)^2}{(0.3)(0.3)} \\ & =\frac{1}{0.09}=11.11 \end{aligned}$$
